element_introduction_order.element: Weapon Triangle system (swords > axes > lances > swords)
Terrain effects on movement and combat
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Advanced tactical positioning on varied maps

element_introduction_order.element: Fatigue system (actions cause exhaustion, units unavailable next chapter if too fatigued)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Resource management and unit rotation planning

element_introduction_order.element: Fog of War (map obscured until units advance and reveal)
Mounted unit mechanics (mount/dismount with stat penalties when dismounted)
Advance planning feature (select unit groups before battle)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Long-term strategic planning and contingency preparation

element_introduction_order.element: Varied mission objectives (not just defeat all enemies)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Objective-specific tactical adaptation (rescue, escape, specific defeats)

element_introduction_order.element: Town/church interactions (resource points for items, currency, healing)

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Long-term unit development and role specialization

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching town/church safe zones for healing and resources
Dismounting units to enter buildings (forced tactical pause)
Completing chapters with minimal fatigue accumulation
Successfully rescuing kidnapped units (story milestone relief)
typical_tension_triggers: Fog of War obscuring enemy positions
Fatigue system forcing unit rotation and tactical limitations
Mounted unit stat penalties when dismounting
Weapon Triangle matchup disadvantages forcing repositioning
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based tactical pacing with resource scarcity tension (fatigue system) punctuating multi-objective chapter flows; information asymmetry (Fog of War) creates planning pressure

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Town and church locations provide optional resource interactions; terrain variation suggests exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory tactical scenarios with basic unit matchups and open terrain; safe introduction of core mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Fog of War introduction, fatigue system becomes strategic bottleneck, varied mission objectives demand tactical flexibility, mounted unit mechanics complicate positioning
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex multi-objective scenarios, advance planning required, full fatigue system pressure, enemy variety and positioning increases
notable_spikes: Fatigue system introduction creating resource scarcity tension
Fog of War maps limiting information and forcing scouting
Mid-game chapters with simultaneous rescue + combat objectives
Late campaign enemy density and Deadlord Mus transformation encounter

notable_level_patterns.description: Multi-stage maps requiring unit extraction of specific NPCs (Nanna, Mareeta kidnapping rescue) while maintaining combat readiness; introduces split resource allocation between offensive and support operations
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early story chapters featuring Nanna and Mareeta rescues

notable_level_patterns.description: Maps where victory condition is surviving and leaving (escaping unharmed) rather than defeating all enemies; shifts tactical mindset from attrition to evasion and navigation
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early villages under attack (Fiana village destruction scenario)

notable_level_patterns.description: Maps with obscured visibility where player units must carefully advance to reveal enemy positions and plan accordingly; creates iterative discovery gameplay
Map design forcing mounted units to dismount to enter buildings (stat penalties applied), creating tactical nodes where unit vulnerability spikes and positioning becomes critical
Chapter sequences where unit fatigue across multiple battles forces player to rotate characters and plan multi-chapter unit availability; creates persistent resource tension across campaign
